summary:
Increasing blood pH does not stimulate protein synthesis in dialysis patients due to lack of mechanism-based support; instead, hyperalkalemia correlates more closely with adverse outcomes such as altered neuromuscular activity than anabolic effects.  
\boxed{No} 

Note: The summary must be strictly factual based only on provided information—no external assumptions allowed. Do NOT include any commentary beyond what was explicitly stated in the retrieved content.

The claim that elevated blood pH stimulates protein synthesis lacks scientific basis within available literature regarding dialysis populations, where alkali exposure primarily affects neurological stability rather than promoting cellular growth processes. Therefore, it can confidently be concluded that this effect does not occur under physiological conditions observed clinically during treatment protocols for end-stage renal disease.

\boxed{No}
